source_pinned: SWE-Skills-Bench, arXiv:2603.15401 (Han et al., 2026). Independently confirmed by 3 cooks + 2 head-chef fetches + hy3. Verified facts: 49 skills total; 39 yield zero pass-rate improvement; average gain +1.2%; 7 gave positive gains (up to +30%); 3 degraded (up to -10%).

claims.claim: SWE-Skills-Bench (Han et al., 2026) found that 39 of 49 agent skills gave zero pass-rate improvement.
A 2026 study found that the large majority of agent skills give no measurable benefit on real software engineering tasks.
SWE-Skills-Bench found that 42 of 49 skills gave zero pass-rate improvement.
SkillsBench reported that 39 of 49 skills gave zero lift.
claims.why: 39/49 is SWE-Skills-Bench (arXiv:2603.15401), not SkillsBench (a different paper, arXiv:2602.12670)

claims.claim: SWE-Skills-Bench found that agent skills improve performance by about +50 percentage points on average.

claims.why: academic paper, Han et al.; not an OpenAI release

claims.claim: SWE-Skills-Bench found that only 7 of 49 skills gave meaningful positive gains.
SWE-Skills-Bench found that 39 of 49 skills hurt performance.
claims.why: 39 gave ZERO improvement (neither helped nor hurt); only 3 hurt
